mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0 percent, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts and consumer credit simultaneously rising indicates synchronized real-economy and financial demand expansion. This joint acceleration lifts equity cash flow projections and valuation premiums, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both rise month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy in more than 55 percent of out-of-sample quarters, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a month-over-month increase in the industrial production index signals rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization. This supply-side improvement reduces input cost inflation and supports immediate equity risk appetite, yielding positive 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain flat or negative when INDPRO expands during supply chain bottlenecks, or the signal loses predictive power once CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds 3.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth rate reflects underlying aggregate demand strength that directly supports corporate earnings revisions. This fundamental momentum translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month correlation between GDPC1 revisions and 5-day SPX returns drops below 0.10, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero across all quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a 2.5 percent year-over-year growth threshold, persistent inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy. Higher discount rates and compressed earnings multiples subsequently drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally for more than 65 percent of the time following CPIAUCSL breaches of the 2.5 percent threshold, or the signal exhibits a negative rank IC over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth acceleration and unemployment rate decline confirm a self-reinforcing labor market expansion. This dual improvement validates sustained consumer demand and corporate profit growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 100k and UNRATE falls > 0.2pp in same month
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 60 percent of observed periods, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion environments, a quarterly real GDP growth rate exceeding 2.0 percent signals overheating demand that eventually triggers aggressive monetary tightening. This anticipated policy pivot compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth exceeds 2.0 percent
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive forward returns in more than 70 percent of instances where GDPC1 exceeds the 2.0 percent threshold during late-cycle phases, or the signal shows no decay in predictive power over 5-year backtests.
